title: In vitro methods for assessing tissue compatibility of a material
abstract: In some embodiments the present invention provides in vitro methods for assessing a material or device for tissue compatibility, comprising culturing an engineered tissue graft with a test material or device; and determining whether the test material is compatible with the tissue graft. In some embodiments, the present invention provides tissue grafts, such as vascularized bone grafts, and methods for preparing and using such tissue grafts to screen materials and devices for tissue compatibility. In some embodiments the tissue grafts are made using pluripotent stem cells.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An in vitro method for assessing a material for tissue compatibility, the method comprising: a) culturing an engineered tissue graft with a test material; and b) determining whether the test material is compatible with the tissue graft, thereby assessing the material for tissue compatibility. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the tissue graft comprises a scaffold. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the tissue graft comprises cells derived from stem cells or progenitor cells. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the tissue graft comprises cells derived from induced pluripotent stem cells. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the scaffold has a thickness of from about 0.3 millimeters to about 10 millimeters. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the scaffold has a thickness of less than one centimeter. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the scaffold consists essentially of decellularized bone tissue. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the bone tissue is bovine bone tissue. 9. The method of claim 7, wherein the bone tissue is human bone tissue. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the scaffold comprises one or more synthetic materials. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the synthetic material comprises ceramic, cement, polymer composite, or any combination thereof. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein the scaffold has been functionalized. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein the scaffold comprises one or more openings to accommodate the test material. 14. The method of claim 1, wherein the culturing is carried out under static conditions. 15. The method of claim 1, wherein the culturing is carried out under dynamic conditions. 16. The method of claim 1, wherein the culturing is carried out for about 2, about 3, about 4, about 5, about 6, about 7, about 8, about 9, or about 10 weeks. 17. The method of claim 1, wherein the culturing is carried out for more than 10 weeks. 18. The method of claim 1, wherein the culturing is carried out in a culture vessel. 19. The method of claim 1, wherein the culture vessel comprises a bioreactor, a spinner flask, a rotating vessel, a perfusion or compressive system, or any combination thereof. 20. The method of claim 1, wherein the tissue graft is a bone graft. 21. The method of claim 1, wherein the tissue graft is vascularized. 22. The method of claim 1 wherein the tissue graft comprises two or more cell types. 23. The method of claim 1, wherein the method is carried out in a high throughput format. 24. he method of claim 1, wherein the test material is a device, or a portion of a device. 25. The method of claim 1, wherein the device is a medical device. 26. The method of claim 1, wherein (b) comprises determining a molecular and/or biological response of the tissue to the test material. 27. The method of claim 1, wherein (b) comprises determining one or more of the following: a) integration of the test material with the tissue graft; b) amount and/or quality of the tissue graft; c) interaction of the test material with the tissue graft; d) migration of cells to and/or on and/or around the test material; e) cell attachment, cell morphology, cell survival and/or cell proliferation; f) gene and/or protein expression and/or release in the tissue graft; g) strength of the interaction of the test material with the tissue graft; or h) biomechanics of the test material. 28. The method of claim 1, wherein the determining is by computed tomography (CT), microtomography (microCT), microscopy, electron microscopy, scanning electron microscopy, immunohistochemistry, histology, or any combination thereof. 29. A system for assessing tissue compatibility of a material, the system comprising: a) an engineered tissue graft; and b) a test material, wherein the tissue graft is cultured in vitro with the test material to determine whether the test material is compatible with the tissue. 30. The system of claim 29, wherein the tissue graft comprises a scaffold. 31. The system of claim 29, wherein the tissue graft comprises cells derived from stem cells or progenitor cells. 32. The system of claim 29, wherein the tissue graft comprises cells derived from induced pluripotent stem cells. 33. The system of claim 29, wherein the tissue graft comprises more than one type of tissue forming cell. 34. The system of claim 29, wherein the scaffold has a thickness of from about 0.3 millimeters to about 10 millimeters. 35. The system of claim 29, wherein the scaffold has a thickness of less than one centimeter. 36. The system of claim 29, wherein the scaffold consists essentially of decellularized bone tissue. 37. The system of claim 36, wherein the bone tissue is bovine bone tissue. 38. The system of claim 36, wherein the bone tissue is human bone tissue. 39. The system of claim 29, wherein the scaffold comprises one or more synthetic materials. 40. The system of claim 39, wherein the synthetic material comprises ceramic, cement, polymer composite, or any combination thereof. 41. The system of claim 29, wherein the scaffold and/or test material is functionalized. 42. The system of claim 29, wherein the scaffold comprises one or more openings to accommodate the test material. 43. The system of claim 29, wherein the in vitro culture is carried out under static conditions. 44. The system of claim 29, wherein the in vitro culture is carried out under dynamic conditions. 45. The system of claim 29, wherein the in vitro culture is carried out for about 2, about 3, about 4, about 5, about 6, about 7, about 8, about 9, or about 10 weeks. 46. The system of claim 29, wherein the in vitro culture is carried out for more than 10 weeks. 47. The system of claim 29, wherein the in vitro culture is carried out in a culture vessel. 48. The system of claim 47, wherein the culture vessel comprises a bioreactor, a spinner flask, a rotating vessel, a perfusion or compressive system, or any combination thereof. 49. The system of claim 29, wherein the tissue graft is a bone graft. 50. The system of claim 29, wherein the tissue graft is vascularized. 51. The system of claim 29, wherein the tissue graft comprises more than one type of tissue forming cell. 52. The system of claim 29, wherein the system is in a high throughput screening format. 53. The system of claim 29, wherein the test material is a device, or a portion of a device. 54. The system of claim 29, wherein the device is a medical device. 55. The system of claim 29, wherein determining whether the test material is compatible with the tissue comprises assessing a molecular and/or biological response of the tissue to the test material. 56. The system of claim 29, wherein determining whether the test material is compatible with the tissue comprises assessing one or more of the following: a) integration of the test material with the tissue graft; b) amount and/or quality of the tissue graft; c) interaction of the test material with the tissue graft; d) migration of cells to and/or on and/or around the test material; e) cell attachment, cell morphology, cell survival and/or cell proliferation; f) gene and/or protein expression and/or release in the tissue graft; g) strength of the interaction of the test material with the tissue graft; or h) biomechanics of the test material. 57. The system of claim 29, wherein determining whether the test material is compatible with the tissue is by computed tomography (CT), microtomography (microCT), microscopy, electron microscopy, scanning electron microscopy, immunohistochemistry, histology, or any combination thereof. 58. A kit for assessing tissue compatibility of a test material, the kit comprising: a) one or more populations of tissue forming cells; b) two or more scaffolds; and c) one or more control materials. 59. The kit of claim 58, wherein each population of tissue forming cells is pre-applied to a scaffold. 60. The kit of claim 58, wherein the control material is titanium. 61. The kit of claim 58, wherein at least one population of cells comprises cells derived from stem cells or progenitor cells. 62. The kit of claim 58, wherein at least one population of cells comprises cells derived from induced pluripotent stem cells. 63. The kit of claim 58, wherein each scaffold has a thickness of from about 0.3 millimeters to about 10 millimeters. 64. The kit of claim 58, wherein each scaffold has a thickness of less than one centimeter. 65. The kit of claim 58, wherein each scaffold consists essentially of decellularized bone tissue. 66. The kit of claim 58, wherein the scaffold is functionalized. 67. The kit of claim 65, wherein the bone tissue is bovine bone tissue. 68. The kit of claim 65, wherein the bone tissue is human bone tissue. 69. The kit of claim 58, wherein the scaffold comprises one or more synthetic materials. 70. The kit of claim 69, wherein the synthetic material comprises ceramic, cement, polymer composite, or any combination thereof. 71. The kit of claim 58, wherein the scaffold comprises one or more openings to accommodate the test material. 72. A method of preparing a tissue graft, the method comprising culturing one or more populations of cells on a scaffold to form a tissue graft, wherein the tissue graft is cultured in vitro with a test material to determine whether the test material is compatible with the tissue. 73. The method of claim 72, wherein the tissue graft is a bone graft. 74. The method of claim 72, wherein the tissue graft is a cartilage graft. 75. The method of claim 72, wherein the tissue graft is vascularized. 76. The method of claim 72, wherein the tissue graft has a thickness of about one centimeter or less. 77. The method of claim 72, wherein the tissue graft has a thickness of from about 0.3 millimeters to about 10 millimeters. 78. The method of claim 72, wherein the scaffold is generated using computer assisted manufacturing, three-dimensional printing, casting, milling, laser cutting, rapid prototyping, or any combination thereof. 79. The method of claim 72, wherein the cells are derived from induced pluripotent stem cells. 80. The method of claim 72, wherein the cells comprise bone-forming cells and/or cells capable of differentiating into bone-forming cells. 81. The method of claim 72, wherein the cells comprise blood vessel-forming cells and/or cells capable of differentiating into blood vessel-forming cells. 82. The method of claim 72, wherein the cells comprise mesenchymal progenitor cells. 83. The method of claim 72, wherein the cells comprise endothelial progenitor cells. 84. An in vitro method for assessing a material for tissue compatibility, the method comprising: a) preparing a tissue graft by: i) obtaining a digital model of a tissue portion to be produced, repaired, or replaced, wherein the digital model is partitioned into two or more model segments; ii) preparing two or more tissue graft segments, wherein each tissue graft segment has a size and shape corresponding to that of a model segment of step (a); and iii) assembling the two or more tissue graft segments prepared in step (b) to form a tissue graft, wherein the tissue graft has a size and shape corresponding to that of the tissue portion of step (a); b) culturing the tissue graft with a test material; and c) determining whether the test material is compatible with the tissue graft. 85. The method of claim 84, wherein preparing the two or more tissue graft segments in (b) comprises: a) obtaining a scaffold, wherein the scaffold has a size and shape corresponding to a model segment; b) obtaining a culture vessel comprising a graft chamber, wherein the graft chamber has a size and shape corresponding to the model segment so as to accommodate the scaffold of (a); c) applying one or more populations of cells to the scaffold; and d) culturing the cells on the scaffold within the culture vessel to form a tissue graft segment. 86. The method of claim 84, wherein the tissue graft is a bone graft. 87. The method of claim 84, wherein the tissue graft is a cartilage graft. 88. The method of claim 84, wherein the tissue graft is vascularized. 89. The method of claim 84, wherein each tissue graft segment has a thickness of about one centimeter or less. 90. The method of claim 84, wherein each tissue graft segment has a thickness of from about 0.3 millimeters to about 10 millimeters. 91. The method of claim 85, wherein the culture vessel is a direct perfusion bioreactor and the culturing of (d) is carried out under press-fit conditions. 92. The method of claim 85, wherein the graft chamber of (b) comprises a graft chamber insert. 93. The method of claim 85, wherein the scaffold is generated using computer assisted manufacturing, three-dimensional printing, casting, milling, laser cutting, rapid prototyping, or any combination thereof. 94. The method of claim 85, wherein the cells are derived from induced pluripotent stem cells. 95. The method of claim 85, wherein the cells comprise bone-forming cells and/or cells capable of differentiating into bone-forming cells. 96. The method of claim 85, wherein the cells comprise blood vessel-forming cells and/or cells capable of differentiating into blood vessel-forming cells. 97. The method of claim 85, wherein the cells comprise mesenchymal progenitor cells. 98. The method of claim 85, wherein the cells comprise endothelial progenitor cells. 99. The method of claim 84, wherein the assembling of (c) comprises a using a biocompatible adhesive, stitches, sutures, staples, plates, pins, screws, or any combination thereof. 100. A tissue graft comprising two or more tissue graft segments generated via the method of claim 72, wherein each tissue graft segment has a maximum thickness of about 0.3 millimeters to about 10 millimeters, and wherein the tissue graft segments are assembled together to form a tissue graft using a biocompatible material selected from adhesives, stitches, sutures, staples, plates, pins, screws, or any combination thereof. 101. The tissue graft of claim 100, wherein the graft is a bone graft. 102. The tissue graft of claim 101, wherein the graft is a vascularized bone graft. 103. The tissue graft of claim 100, wherein the graft comprises bone-forming cells derived from induced pluripotent stem cells. 104. The method of claim 1, 72 or 84, wherein culturing is performed in a perfusion bioreactor suitable for use in the preparation of a tissue graft segment, the bioreactor comprising: a) a graft chamber; and b) an equilibration chamber in fluid communication with the graft chamber. 105. The method of claim 104, wherein the graft chamber is configured to accommodate a tissue graft segment. 106. The method of claim 104, wherein the graft chamber further comprises a graft chamber insert configured to accommodate a tissue graft segment. 107. The method of claim 104, comprising a bottom portion and a top portion, the bottom portion comprising: i) the graft chamber, ii) the equilibration chamber, iii) an inlet, and iv) a fluid channel defining a fluid path between the inlet and the equilibration chamber. 108. The method of claim 107, wherein the top portion comprises: i) a fluid reservoir, ii) an aperture fluidly connecting the fluid reservoir and the graft chamber, and iii) an outlet port. 109. The method of claim 104, wherein the bioreactor further comprises an inlet, a fluid channel defining a fluid path between the inlet and the equilibration chamber, a fluid reservoir, an aperture fluidly connecting the fluid reservoir and the graft chamber, the fluid reservoir further comprising an outlet port. 110. The method of claim 109, wherein the graft chamber is disposed adjacent the equilibration chamber. 111. The method of claim 110, further comprising a fluid pump and one or more tubes fluidly connecting the inlet port and the outlet port to the pump thereby providing a fluid circuit. 112. The method of claim 104, wherein the equilibration chamber comprises a flat floor. 113. The method of claim 104, wherein the equilibration chamber comprises a tapered floor. 114. The method of claim 113, wherein the taper forms an angle of about 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60 or 70 degrees between the floor and the equilibration chamber wall. 115. The method of claim 114, wherein the equilibration chamber further comprises one or more diffusion enhancing elements. 116. The method of claim 115, wherein the equilibration chamber further comprises an insert to maintain the dimensions of the equilibration chamber and/or maintain fluid flow through the perfusion bioreactor. 117. The method of claim 109, wherein the graft chamber further comprises an insert to (i) maintain the size and dimensions of the graft chamber and equilibrium chamber, and/or (ii) maintain fluid flow through the perfusion bioreactor. 118. The method of any of claims 1, 72 or 84, further comprising contacting the tissue graft with a test agent and assessing compatibility of the test material with the tissue graft. 119. The method of claim 118, wherein determining whether the test material is compatible with the tissue comprises assessing one or more of the following: a) integration of the test material with the tissue graft; b) amount and/or quality of the tissue graft; c) interaction of the test material with the tissue graft; d) migration of cells to and/or on and/or around the test material; e) cell attachment, cell morphology, cell survival and/or cell proliferation; f) gene and/or protein expression and/or release in the tissue graft; g) strength of the interaction of the test material with the tissue graft; or h) biomechanics of the test material. 120. The method of claim 118, wherein the test agent is a small molecule, a peptide, a nucleic acid molecule, or a protein. 121. The method of claim 120, wherein the nucleic acid molecule is an siRNA, shRNA, miRNA, Locked Nucleic Acid (LNA), antisense oligonucleotide, a chemically modified oligonucleotide, or a combination thereof.
